mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, which translates into higher consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening economy, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in a downturn.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 10-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also high, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle aimed at curbing inflation, which typically constrains economic activity and leads to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:high rates and inflation are challenging for equities
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL is above 220 and FEDFUNDS is above 0.15, yet the S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in over 50% of these periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable Federal Funds Rate signals an accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital for businesses and encouraging investment, which supports higher equity valuations over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates support asset prices
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.20 for 6 consecutive months, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average during this period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production indicates a slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic output, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and profits, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial output is a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines for 3 consecutive months, but the S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of such instances over a 7-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales indicate robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and positive market sentiment, driving S&P 500 forward returns in the very short term.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major GDP component
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases by more than 0.5% month-over-month, but the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in over 50% of these occurrences, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in housing starts signals a contraction in the construction sector and broader economic weakness, dampening investor confidence and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a key economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST falls below 600.0, but the S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which generally supports corporate earnings and investor optimism, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth correlates with market performance
failure_condition: If the annualized GDPC1 growth rate is above 2.0%, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average over any 3-year period within this regime, the hypothesis is falsified.
